description_long: This object consists of three irregularly shaped stones, each with a pale gray coloration and subtle beige undertones. The stones have a natural, weathered appearance, with rounded edges and uneven surfaces that suggest exposure to the elements. Their surfaces display faint, organic veining and mottling, typical of sedimentary or weathered urban stones. The stones vary in size, with one noticeably larger and flatter than the others, and all exhibit a matte, rough finish. There are no visible carvings, artificial markings, or embedded features, and the stones appear solid and dense. These stones would be commonly found as part of urban landscaping, serving as natural barriers or decorative obstacles in parks, sidewalks, or public plazas.
description: A group of three pale, irregularly shaped stones with rough, weathered surfaces and subtle veining, suitable for urban landscaping or as natural barriers.
description_view_0: Two stones are positioned on the left, one larger and flatter, while a single, more rounded stone sits to the right; surface texture and subtle color variations are visible.
description_view_1: The stones appear more closely grouped, with the largest stone now on the right and the two smaller stones to the left, highlighting their irregular shapes and relative sizes.
description_view_2: All three stones are visible from above, showing their rounded tops and the largest stone's broad, flat surface; surface veining and mottling are most apparent.
description_view_3: The largest stone is in the foreground, with the two smaller stones behind; the irregular, organic contours and rough texture are emphasized.
attribute_location: urban park, sidewalk edge, or landscaped plaza
attribute_placement: set as standalone features or loosely grouped on the ground surface
attribute_pattern: subtle, irregular veining and mottling typical of natural stone; no artificial carvings or regular patterns
attribute_weathering: moderately weathered with rounded edges and a matte, rough surface, indicating prolonged exposure to outdoor elements
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: blends naturally with soil, gravel, or planted areas, providing a soft visual transition between hardscape and greenery
